source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ CE MDD/■ 9. TCF_ADD(HAD1417MCW) 221215 (Rev.4) - PMS Update/Technical Construction File (A-TCF-5-068).docx

document.title: Technical Construction File for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
authorityOrJurisdiction: Council Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC

sourceSummary.oneLine: Technical Construction File A-TCF-5-068 Rev.4 for the ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector under CE MDD requirements, updated for PMS on 2022.12.15.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documentation intended for authorized competent authorities and notified bodies to support CE marking and conformity under Medical Device Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC.
keyPoints: ADD is a Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector for general radiographic diagnosis of adult and children, not for mammography.
The device is classified as Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.
The manufacturer is H&abyz Co.,Ltd. and the EU Representative is JaviTech e.K.

The file lists harmonized standards and supporting attachments for essential requirements, risk management, tests, software, usability, clinical evaluation, declarations, biocompatibility, EC representative agreement, drawings, and translation procedure.

evidence.claim: The document is Technical Construction File A-TCF-5-068 Rev.4 for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical Construction File for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Report No.: A-TCF-5-068 (Rev.4)

evidence.claim: The document revision 4 was a PMS Update dated 2022.12.15.
evidence.evidenceText: 4 PMS Update 2022.12.15

evidence.claim: The document was prepared, reviewed, and approved on Dec 15, 2022.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared Quality team Han Jimin Dec 15, 2022 Review R&D Manager Lee, Dong jin Dec 15, 2022 Approval Quality Manager Hur Namkyu Dec 15, 2022

evidence.claim: The TCF supports CE marking under Medical Device Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC.
evidence.evidenceText: meets the requirements of the Medical Device Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC and which is requested to bear the CE mark

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is H&abyz Co.,Ltd.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer: H&abyz Co.,Ltd.

evidence.claim: The EU Representative is JaviTech e.K.
evidence.evidenceText: EU Representative: JaviTech e.K.

evidence.claim: The ADD device is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD, Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.
evidence.pageTarget: ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The ADD is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The ADD is classified as Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX under MDD 93/42/EEC as amended.
evidence.evidenceText: Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC

evidence.claim: Rule 16 is applicable because devices specifically intended for recording X-ray diagnostic images are Class IIa.
evidence.evidenceText: Rule 16 specifically intended for recording of X-ray diagnostic images are in Class Ⅱa. Class IIa Applicable

evidence.claim: The GMDN code is 61108 Indirect flat panel x-ray detector.
evidence.evidenceText: GMDN Code: 61108 Indirect flat panel x-ray detector

evidence.claim: The relevant essential requirements of Annex I to Council Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC have been applied.
evidence.evidenceText: The relevant essential requirements of Annex I to the Council Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC, have been applied as far as safety and performance-related device features are concerned.

evidence.claim: The ADD uses a-Si TFT sensor technology and CsI scintillator.
evidence.evidenceText: Sensor Type a-Si TFT Scintillator CsI

evidence.claim: The detector model HAD1417MCW has dimensions 384 x 460 x 15 mm and typical weight 3.5 kg.
evidence.evidenceText: Detector Dimension 384 x 460 x 15 mm Weight Typ 3.5 Kg

evidence.claim: The expected service life of ADD is approximately 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD is designed for a service life of approximately 5 years.

evidence.claim: Risk management is conducted in accordance with EN ISO 14971:2019.
evidence.evidenceText: This report is applicable to all stages of the life-cycle in accordance with EN ISO 14971:2019.

evidence.claim: Electrical safety and EMC support are based on EN 60601-1:2006 + AM1:2013 and EN 60601-1-2:2015.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 60601-1:2006 + AM1:2013 Medical electrical equipment, Part 1: General Requirements for Basic Safety and Essential Performance EN 60601-1-2:2015 Medical electrical equipment, Part 1-2 General Requirements for Basic Safety and Essential Performance – Electromagnetic Compatibility

evidence.claim: Performance testing includes EN 62220-1-1:2015 and FDA guidance for 510(k)s for solid state X-ray imaging devices.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62220-1-1:2015 Medical electrical equipment - Characteristics of digital X-ray imaging devices - Part 1: Determination of the detective quantum efficiency FDA Guidance Guidance for the Submission of 510(k)s for Solid State X-ray Imaging Devices
evidence.pageTarget: Solid State X-ray Imaging Devices 510(k) Guidance

evidence.claim: The software is developed and maintained in accordance with EN 62304:2006 + A1:2015.
evidence.evidenceText: The software has been developed and maintained in accordance with EN 62304:2006 + A1:2015

evidence.claim: Usability testing was conducted in accordance with EN 62366-1:2015.
evidence.evidenceText: The usability testing was conducted to demonstrate that the intended users of the ADD can safely and effectively perform critical tasks for the intended uses in the expected use environments in accordance with EN 62366-1:2015.

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation report states clinical performance and safety equivalence to existing commercially available devices.
evidence.evidenceText: The clinical evaluation report demonstrates that the design of ADD is equivalent, from a clinical performance and safety perspective, to one or more existing commercially available devices

evidence.claim: The patient contact part is the front detector deco sheet made of polycarbonate.
evidence.evidenceText: The contact part with patient is front of detector (Deco Sheet) and its material is Poly Carbonate

evidence.claim: The English manual can be translated into the national language of the country upon user request.
evidence.evidenceText: The English manual provided by H&abyz is translated into the national language of the country if the user request.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority signal in source metadata is FDA, but the document is primarily a CE/MDD Technical Construction File; FDA appears only as a performance-data guidance reference.
No notified body name, notified body number, certificate number, or CE certificate status is present in the extracted text.
Some attachment metadata conflicts across the document, including 10-5 listed as Periodic Safety Update Report A-PSUR-004 in one table and Post Market Surveillance Report A-PMS-004 in another.

Some document numbers conflict or appear inconsistent, including Basic Safety, Essential Performance Report A-TCF-5-048 in the attachment list but A-TCF-5-046 in the risk management section, and Vigilance System Procedure AQP808 in one table but A-TCF-5-077 later.
Text contains apparent OCR or source extraction errors such as IEC 62034 Checklist, vivio, car drum, and A-TC F-5-055.
